description_long: This bungalow features a broad, low-pitched hip roof with brown tiling and a prominent overhang, giving it a classic, compact appearance. The exterior walls are painted a muted beige with a dark green band near the top and a red base trim, adding visual interest and grounding the structure. The front facade includes a central entrance flanked by two supporting columns and a small set of steps, with a single window to one side. The side elevations each display a pair of symmetrically placed windows, while the rear mirrors the front with two windows but no entrance. The windows are white-framed with divided panes, contributing to a traditional, homey aesthetic. The overall design is simple yet functional, suitable for residential neighborhoods or suburban settings.
description: A compact bungalow with a brown hip roof, beige walls, red trim, and white-paned windows, featuring a central entrance and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_0: The side view shows two symmetrically placed white-paned windows beneath the broad brown hip roof.
description_view_1: The front view highlights the central entrance with a small porch, flanked by a single window on one side and a wide roof overhang.
description_view_2: The opposite side view mirrors the first, with two white-paned windows and the same roof structure.
description_view_3: The rear view displays a single window offset to one side, with no entrance and a continuous roof overhang.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical side windows, single front and rear windows
